title: Arithmetic operators should only have numbers as operands
description: Arithmetic operators should only have numbers as operandsAnalyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode Smellconfusingtype-dependentWhy is this an issue?Expressions with arithmetic (/, *, %, ++, --, -, -=, *=, /=, %=, +=, +), unary (-), or comparison operators (>, <, >=, <=) where one, or both, of the operands is a String, Boolean or Date value rely on implicit conversions. Both the maintainability and reliability levels of such a piece of code are questionable.Noncompliant code examplestr = "80"; quarter = str / 4; // Noncompliant if (str < 10) { // Noncompliant // ... }Compliant solutionstr = "80"; parsedStr = parseInt(str); quarter = parsedStr / 4; if (parsedStr < 10) { // ..
recommendation: Ensure arithmetic operators are used only with numeric operands to avoid type coercion.
impact: Type coercion bugs and unclear behavior

checks.message: Arithmetic operations should use numeric operands only
